enrichment.short_description: Email marketing automation platform built for SaaS companies, with account-level segmentation, visual workflows, and in-app messaging.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Userlist is email automation software for SaaS teams: visual workflows, account-level segmentation, in-app messages, and a 14-day free trial.

features.title: Visual workflows
features.description: Drag-and-drop builder for multi-step automated customer journeys.

features.title: Account-level automation
features.description: Handles many-to-many relationships between users and companies, not just individual contacts.

features.title: Dynamic segmentation
features.description: User- and company-level segments built from behavioral and custom event data.

features.title: Transactional email
features.description: Turns receipts and notifications into marketing-aware messages.

features.title: In-app messages
features.description: Targeted notifications delivered inside the product itself.

features.title: A/B testing
features.description: Runs A/B/C tests across different workflow paths.

features.title: Conversion goals
features.description: Tracks behavioral outcomes achieved within a workflow.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: 70+ integrations including Slack, HubSpot, Salesforce, Stripe, Zapier, and Make.

pros: Built specifically for B2B SaaS account-based messaging rather than generic email blasts
Combines lifecycle email, in-app messages, and transactional email in one tool
70+ native integrations plus webhook support
14-day free trial with no credit card required
cons: No permanent free plan, only a 14-day trial
Exact starting price is not published and requires visiting the pricing calculator
Advanced features like A/B testing and conversion goals are limited to the Professional tier and above
SDKs are limited to JavaScript, Ruby, PHP, and Laravel

faqs.question: Is there a free plan?
faqs.answer: No, Userlist offers a 14-day free trial with no credit card required rather than a permanent free tier.

faqs.question: Which plans are available?
faqs.answer: Basic, Professional, and a custom Enterprise plan, priced by number of users with per-1,000-user increments.

faqs.question: Does Userlist support in-app messaging?
faqs.answer: Yes, in-app messages and team notifications are included on all plans.

faqs.question: What integrations does Userlist offer?
faqs.answer: 70+ integrations including Slack, HubSpot, Salesforce, Stripe, and Zapier, plus webhooks.

use_cases.title: Lifecycle email automation
use_cases.description: Build onboarding, trial-conversion, and retention journeys triggered by product usage.

use_cases.title: Account-based messaging
use_cases.description: Segment and message at the company level when multiple users belong to one account.

features: Up to 10,000 users included, then $10/month per additional 1,000 users
Up to 10,000 users included, then $12/month per additional 1,000 users

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Userlist?
paragraphs: Userlist is an email marketing automation platform built specifically for SaaS companies. It lets teams build visual, multi-step customer journeys that combine lifecycle email, transactional email, and in-app messages.
Unlike generic email tools, Userlist models many-to-many relationships between users and companies, so messaging can be targeted at the account level rather than just individual contacts.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Userlist is for
paragraphs: Userlist is aimed at growing B2B SaaS companies that need behavior-driven onboarding, retention, and upsell messaging tied to product usage data.

enrichment.short_description: Email platform for SaaS companies that unifies product, marketing, and transactional email sending with a contact-based pricing model.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Loops is email software for SaaS teams: marketing, product, and transactional email in one tool, with a free plan up to 1,000 contacts.

features.title: Cross-client email editor
features.description: Email design tool built for consistent rendering across email clients.

features.title: Lifecycle workflows
features.description: Automation builder with triggers, timers, and branching/conditional logic.

features.title: Dynamic segmentation
features.description: Segments synced with product data using contact properties and engagement metrics.

features.title: Conversion analytics
features.description: Tracks outcomes beyond open rates, including upgrades and revenue attribution.

features.title: Developer API
features.description: REST API for contacts and events, plus an npm package for programmatic sending.

features.title: No per-seat pricing
features.description: Loops does not charge extra for additional team seats.

pros: Permanent free plan for up to 1,000 contacts and 4,000 sends/month, no credit card required
Transactional email is included at no extra charge on paid plans
No per-seat charges, so teams can add users without added cost
Native integrations with developer-focused tools like Supabase, Clerk, and Segment
cons: Free plan emails carry a Loops branding footer
Exact paid plan prices are not listed publicly and require using an interactive pricing slider
Free plan is capped at 1,000 subscribed contacts and 4,000 sends/month

faqs.question: Is there a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Loops offers a free plan for up to 1,000 subscribed contacts and about 4,000 sends per month, no credit card required.

faqs.question: Does Loops charge per team seat?
faqs.answer: No, Loops does not charge for additional team seats on any plan.

faqs.question: Is transactional email included?
faqs.answer: Yes, transactional email sending is included at no additional charge on paid plans.

faqs.question: What integrations does Loops support?
faqs.answer: Supabase, Clerk, Zapier, Framer, Make, Segment, Webflow, Bubble, Stripe, PostHog, and Auth0, plus webhooks.

use_cases.title: Unified SaaS email sending
use_cases.description: Send marketing campaigns, lifecycle emails, and transactional messages from a single platform.

use_cases.title: Developer-driven email
use_cases.description: Trigger and manage emails programmatically via API or npm package.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Loops?
paragraphs: Loops is an email platform for SaaS companies that combines product, marketing, and transactional email in a single tool, using contact-based rather than send-based pricing.
It provides a lifecycle workflow builder with triggers, timers, and branching logic, alongside a developer API and npm package for programmatic sending.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Loops is for
paragraphs: Loops targets SaaS teams and technical founders who want marketing and transactional email managed together, with direct access to product/usage data for segmentation.

enrichment.short_description: Developer-focused email API for sending transactional and marketing email programmatically, with SDKs for nine languages.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Resend is a developer email API with a RESTful API, SMTP relay, React Email support, and a free plan for up to 3,000 emails/month.

features.title: REST API
features.description: Core email sending via a simple, modern REST API.

features.title: SMTP relay
features.description: Send email over standard SMTP alongside the API.

features.title: React Email support
features.description: Build emails as React components.

features.title: Multi-language SDKs
features.description: Official libraries for Node.js, Python, PHP, Ruby, Go, Java, Laravel, Rust, and .NET.

features.title: CLI tool
features.description: Command-line interface for terminal-based email operations.

features.title: MCP server
features.description: Model Context Protocol server for integrating email sending into AI clients like Cursor and Claude.

features.title: Batch sending and tracking
features.description: Batch email sending with open and link tracking.

features.title: Inbound email
features.description: Receive and process inbound email in addition to sending.

pros: Free tier includes 3,000 emails/month with no time limit
Broad SDK coverage across nine programming languages
Built-in MCP server for AI agent/assistant integration
SOC 2 Type II and GDPR compliance included on all plans
cons: Free plan is capped at 100 emails/day and 1 domain
Overage charges apply once monthly volume is exceeded
Marketing email (contact-based) and transactional email are priced as separate plan tracks
Enterprise-scale volume (3M+ emails/month) requires custom/quoted pricing

faqs.question: Is there a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Resend's free plan includes 3,000 emails/month (up to 100/day), 1 domain, and 5 AI credits/month.

faqs.question: What does the Pro plan cost?
faqs.answer: Pro plans start at $20/month for 50,000 emails/month, with a $35/month tier for 100,000 emails/month.

faqs.question: Does Resend support React-based email templates?
faqs.answer: Yes, Resend supports React Email for building emails as components.

faqs.question: Can AI agents use Resend?
faqs.answer: Yes, Resend provides an MCP server for integration with AI clients such as Cursor and Claude, and plans include a monthly allotment of AI credits.

use_cases.title: Transactional email for apps
use_cases.description: Send receipts, notifications, and password resets via API or SMTP.

use_cases.title: Marketing email for products
use_cases.description: Send contact-based marketing campaigns using the same platform.

use_cases.title: AI agent email sending
use_cases.description: Let AI agents send email through an MCP server integration.

features: Custom automation runs, retention, and domains

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Resend?
paragraphs: Resend is an email API platform for developers to send transactional and marketing email programmatically, via REST API or SMTP relay.
It offers SDKs across nine languages, React Email support for component-based templates, and an MCP server so AI clients like Cursor and Claude can send email directly.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Resend is for
paragraphs: Resend is built for software developers and teams embedding email sending into applications, as well as builders integrating email into AI agent workflows.

enrichment.short_description: Transactional email delivery service with API and SMTP sending, message streams, templates, and inbound email handling.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Postmark is a transactional email API and SMTP service with message streams, templates, and a free plan for 100 emails/month.

features.title: Email API and SMTP
features.description: Send transactional email programmatically via API or SMTP relay.

features.title: Message streams
features.description: Separates transactional from broadcast/promotional email traffic for better deliverability.

features.title: Email templates
features.description: Reusable templates for standardized transactional messages.

features.title: Inbound email
features.description: Receive and process inbound email through the platform.

features.title: Bulk API
features.description: API designed for large-scale sending.

features.title: Analytics and retention
features.description: Delivery analytics with configurable data retention up to 365 days on higher plans.

features.title: Webhooks
features.description: Event notifications for delivery, bounces, and opens.

pros: Free plan never expires, with 100 emails/month for testing or side projects
Focused specifically on transactional email deliverability
Message streams keep transactional and broadcast email separated
Official libraries for Node.js, Python, Ruby on Rails, Ruby, PHP, and C#
cons: Free plan is limited to 100 emails/month
Longer data retention (up to 365 days) requires a higher-tier plan
Per-email overage rates apply once plan volume is exceeded
Primarily transactional-only; not a marketing campaign builder

faqs.question: Is there a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Postmark's free plan never expires and includes 100 emails/month.

faqs.question: What does the entry paid plan cost?
faqs.answer: The Basic plan starts at $15/month for 10,000 emails/month, with overage at $1.80 per 1,000 emails.

faqs.question: Who owns Postmark?
faqs.answer: Postmark is owned by ActiveCampaign.

faqs.question: Does Postmark support inbound email?
faqs.answer: Yes, inbound email processing is available starting on paid plans.

use_cases.title: Transactional email delivery
use_cases.description: Send receipts, password resets, and notifications with reliable inbox delivery.

use_cases.title: Deliverability testing
use_cases.description: Use the free plan to test integration and evaluate deliverability before scaling.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Postmark?
paragraphs: Postmark is a transactional email delivery service that sends email via API or SMTP, with a focus on fast inbox delivery for receipts, password resets, and account notifications.
It separates transactional and broadcast traffic using Message Streams, and provides templates, inbound email handling, and delivery analytics.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Postmark is for
paragraphs: Postmark suits developers and companies of any size that need reliable transactional email infrastructure, from side projects on the free plan to larger senders on paid tiers.

enrichment.short_description: Email delivery platform offering send, validate, and deliverability-optimization APIs and SMTP relay for developers.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Mailgun is an email API and SMTP platform for sending, validating, and optimizing transactional email, used by over 150,000 businesses.

features.title: Send
features.description: Email API and SMTP relay for transactional email delivery.

features.title: Optimize
features.description: Deliverability tools to improve inbox placement and engagement.

features.title: Validate
features.description: Real-time and bulk email address validation.

features.title: Inspect
features.description: Email preview and testing tools before sending.

features.title: Inbound routing
features.description: Route and process inbound email.

features.title: Send time optimization
features.description: Available on higher tiers to improve delivery timing.

pros: Free plan with 100 emails/day and no time limit
Used by over 150,000 businesses, including large tech companies
Combines sending, validation, and deliverability tools in one platform
Part of the Sinch Email suite alongside Mailjet and Email on Acid
cons: Free plan capped at 100 emails/day
SAML SSO and dedicated IP pools require the higher-tier Scale plan
Foundation and Scale plans' listed prices apply after a 1-month trial period
Enterprise-level features require custom/quoted pricing

faqs.question: Is there a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Mailgun offers a free plan with 100 emails per day, 1 sending domain, and 2 API keys.

faqs.question: What does the entry paid plan cost?
faqs.answer: The Basic plan starts at $15/month for 10,000 emails/month with no daily limit.

faqs.question: Does Mailgun offer email validation?
faqs.answer: Yes, real-time and bulk email validation are available, with a validation allotment included on the Scale plan.

faqs.question: Who owns Mailgun?
faqs.answer: Mailgun is part of Sinch's email product suite, alongside Mailjet and Email on Acid.

use_cases.title: Transactional email sending
use_cases.description: Send application email via API or SMTP at scale.

use_cases.title: Email list validation
use_cases.description: Validate email addresses in real time or in bulk before sending.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Mailgun?
paragraphs: Mailgun is an email delivery platform that lets developers send, validate, and optimize transactional email through APIs and SMTP relay.
Beyond sending, it offers real-time and bulk email validation and deliverability tooling, and is used by more than 150,000 businesses.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Mailgun is for
paragraphs: Mailgun targets developers and businesses of all sizes that need programmatic email sending, from startups on the free plan to enterprises needing dedicated IPs and SSO.

enrichment.short_description: Twilio-owned email delivery platform offering an Email API/SMTP service for developers and Marketing Campaigns for marketers.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SendGrid (Twilio) is an email API and marketing campaigns platform sending over 200 billion emails a month, with a free trial available.

features.title: Email API and SMTP
features.description: Send transactional emails programmatically via API or SMTP.

features.title: Marketing Campaigns
features.description: Tools for marketers to create and manage email marketing campaigns.

features.title: Proven deliverability
features.description: Infrastructure sending more than 200 billion emails per month.

features.title: Twilio platform integration
features.description: Connects with Twilio's broader customer engagement platform, including SMS, voice, and identity verification.

pros: Handles very high email volume (200+ billion emails/month across the platform)
Serves both developers (Email API) and marketers (Marketing Campaigns) from one provider
Free trial available with no credit card required
Integrates with Twilio's SMS, voice, and identity products
cons: Email API and Marketing Campaigns are priced and sold as separate products
Exact plan prices are not consistently published on the current site
Now fully folded into Twilio's site and branding, which can complicate finding SendGrid-specific info

faqs.question: Is SendGrid still a separate product?
faqs.answer: SendGrid is now part of Twilio, with its marketing consolidated onto the Twilio site, though the Email API and Marketing Campaigns products remain available.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, SendGrid offers a free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: What are the two main SendGrid products?
faqs.answer: Email API/SMTP for developers sending transactional email, and Marketing Campaigns for marketers running email campaigns.

use_cases.title: Transactional email at scale
use_cases.description: Send application email via API or SMTP with high-volume deliverability infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Email marketing campaigns
use_cases.description: Create and manage customer email campaigns using Marketing Campaigns.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is SendGrid?
paragraphs: SendGrid is an email delivery and marketing platform, now operating under Twilio, offering both an Email API/SMTP service for developers and a separate Marketing Campaigns product for marketers.
The platform is used to send more than 200 billion emails per month and integrates with Twilio's wider customer engagement tools such as SMS and voice.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who SendGrid is for
paragraphs: SendGrid serves developers who need transactional email infrastructure and marketing teams running customer email campaigns, particularly those already using or considering Twilio's broader platform.

enrichment.short_description: Email marketing and transactional sending platform with an AI template designer, landing pages, and paid newsletter tools.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Elastic Email offers email marketing, transactional sending, and an AI template designer, with a free plan for up to 1,000 contacts.

features.title: AI Template Designer
features.description: Generates email templates from a described concept while maintaining brand consistency.

features.title: Email marketing campaigns
features.description: Campaign design, subscriber management, and automated workflows.

features.title: Email API and SMTP
features.description: Programmatic and SMTP-based transactional email sending.

features.title: Landing pages and signup forms
features.description: Build landing pages and forms to grow subscriber lists.

features.title: Paid newsletters
features.description: Monetize newsletters directly through the platform.

features.title: Email verification
features.description: Validate email addresses to maintain list quality.

pros: Free plan includes 1,000 contacts and 3,000 emails/month
AI Template Designer generates on-brand templates automatically
Combines marketing email, transactional API/SMTP, and paid newsletters in one product
Integrates with HubSpot, Shopify, WordPress, WooCommerce, and Make
cons: Starter and Pro plans require a credit card, unlike the Free plan
Starter and Pro plans share the same 2,500-contact/37,500-email allowance at different price points
Enterprise-scale accounts (1M+ contacts) require custom pricing

faqs.question: Is there a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan includes up to 1,000 contacts and 3,000 emails/month with no credit card required.

faqs.question: What does the Starter plan cost?
faqs.answer: The Starter plan is $29/month for up to 2,500 contacts and 37,500 emails/month.

faqs.question: Does Elastic Email offer AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, its AI Template Designer converts a described concept into a styled, brand-consistent email template.

faqs.question: Can I sell newsletters through Elastic Email?
faqs.answer: Yes, paid newsletter monetization is available on the Pro plan.

use_cases.title: AI-assisted email design
use_cases.description: Generate on-brand email templates from a text description.

use_cases.title: Transactional + marketing email
use_cases.description: Send both transactional API/SMTP email and marketing campaigns from one account.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Elastic Email?
paragraphs: Elastic Email is an email platform combining marketing campaigns, transactional sending via API/SMTP, landing pages, and paid newsletter tools.
It includes an AI Template Designer that converts a described concept into a styled email template, and integrates with tools like HubSpot, Shopify, and WordPress.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Elastic Email is for
paragraphs: Elastic Email is built for growing businesses and developers that want email marketing and transactional sending in one place without a large budget or team.

enrichment.short_description: Straightforward email marketing platform with a drag-and-drop editor, automation, and a permanent free plan for up to 2,500 subscribers.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: EmailOctopus is affordable email marketing software with a drag-and-drop editor, automation, and a free plan for up to 2,500 subscribers.

features.title: Drag-and-drop editor
features.description: Build campaigns with images, video blocks, and custom code.

features.title: Automation
features.description: Triggered email workflows based on subscriber actions.

features.title: Segmentation
features.description: Segment subscribers using tags and custom fields.

features.title: Personalization
features.description: Dynamic subject lines and content per recipient.

features.title: Forms and landing pages
features.description: Create signup forms and landing pages to grow subscriber lists.

features.title: 1,000+ integrations
features.description: Connects with over 1,000 third-party applications.

pros: Permanent free plan for up to 2,500 subscribers and 10,000 emails/month, no credit card required
Pro plan starts at $9/month, cheaper than several mainstream competitors
1,000+ third-party integrations
20% lifetime discount available for nonprofits
cons: Free plan emails carry EmailOctopus branding
Free plan reports are only retained for 30 days versus indefinitely on Pro
Free plan is limited to 1 landing page, 1 form, and 1 user
Accounts over 500,000 subscribers require custom pricing

faqs.question: Is there a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Starter plan is free for up to 2,500 subscribers and 10,000 emails/month, no credit card required.

faqs.question: What does the Pro plan cost?
faqs.answer: The Pro plan starts at $9/month billed annually, with unlimited landing pages, forms, and users.

faqs.question: Is there a discount for nonprofits?
faqs.answer: Yes, nonprofits get a 20% lifetime discount on all plans.

faqs.question: Where is EmailOctopus based?
faqs.answer: EmailOctopus is based in London, UK, and operated by Three Hearts Digital Ltd.

use_cases.title: Newsletter sending
use_cases.description: Design and send newsletters with a simple drag-and-drop editor.

use_cases.title: Affordable list growth
use_cases.description: Grow and email a subscriber list on a budget, scaling from the free tier.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is EmailOctopus?
paragraphs: EmailOctopus is an email marketing platform focused on simplicity, offering a drag-and-drop editor, automation, segmentation, and forms/landing pages for growing a subscriber list.
It is based in London, UK, and connects with over 1,000 third-party applications.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who EmailOctopus is for
paragraphs: EmailOctopus suits small to mid-sized businesses, newsletter creators, and other senders who want affordable email marketing without unnecessary complexity.

name: Campaigner
skip_reason: Official site (campaigner.com / www.campaigner.com) could not be fetched — the fetch tool returned an 'unable to fetch' error on repeated attempts across variants, so no first-party facts could be verified.

enrichment.short_description: Email marketing platform with a drag-and-drop builder, contact management, and a free plan for up to 500 contacts.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Benchmark Email offers a drag-and-drop email builder, contact management, and reporting, with a free plan for up to 500 contacts.

features.title: Drag-and-drop email builder
features.description: Design campaigns using professionally designed templates.

features.title: Contact management
features.description: Organize contacts with lists, tags, and segmentation.

features.title: Signup forms
features.description: Forms to grow subscriber lists.

features.title: Real-time reporting
features.description: Contact activity feed and campaign performance reporting.

features.title: Mobile-first design
features.description: Emails optimized for mobile inbox rendering.

features.title: Image library
features.description: Free image assets available within the editor.

pros: Free plan available for up to 500 contacts and 2,500 sends/month with full feature access
Simple drag-and-drop builder suited to non-technical users
Chat and email support included on all plans
Used by large organizations including FedEx, Panasonic, and Samsung
cons: Free plan is capped at 500 contacts and 2,500 monthly sends
Pro plan is limited to 1 included user, with additional users at $15/month each
Enterprise-level pricing (100,000+ contacts) requires a sales consultation

faqs.question: Is there a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Benchmark Email offers a permanent free plan for up to 500 contacts and 2,500 sends/month.

faqs.question: What does the Pro plan cost?
faqs.answer: The Pro plan starts at $27/month, scaling with contact count, and includes 10x the contact limit in monthly sends.

faqs.question: Who owns Benchmark Email?
faqs.answer: Benchmark Email is a registered trademark of Polaris Software, LLC.

faqs.question: Are there extra costs for team members?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Pro plan includes 1 user, with additional users available at $15/month each, up to 10 total.

use_cases.title: Simple campaign sending
use_cases.description: Design and send email campaigns without technical expertise.

use_cases.title: Contact list management
use_cases.description: Organize and segment contacts using lists and tags.
use_cases.audience: Retail, hospitality, and financial services teams

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Benchmark Email?
paragraphs: Benchmark Email is an email marketing platform built around a drag-and-drop builder, contact management, and real-time reporting for creating and tracking campaigns.
It offers a permanent free plan for up to 500 contacts, with paid plans scaling contact limits and send volume.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Benchmark Email is for
paragraphs: Benchmark Email targets small businesses, nonprofits, and organizations in retail, hospitality, manufacturing, and financial services that want simple email marketing without complex features.
